reasoning: The informal statement asserts that every uncountable subset E ⊆ ℝ has a condensation point — a point p such that every neighborhood of p meets E in uncountably many points. The Lean hypothesis `¬ Set.Countable E` matches "E uncountable", and the conclusion `∃ p : ℝ, ∀ U ∈ 𝓝 p, ¬ Set.Countable (U ∩ E)` is precisely the standard definition of "p is a condensation point of E" (every neighborhood of p intersects E in an uncountable set). The quantifier structure (∃ p, ∀ neighborhoods U) matches, p is not required to lie in E (as in the informal statement), and the ambient space is ℝ in both. No hidden assumptions are added or removed.
